messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Introduction to Statistics in R course - online, 19-22 January
messages.subject_clean: Introduction to Statistics in R course - online, 19-22 January
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
start the new year by building strong foundations in  statistics with R!
 
Our online course  "Introduction to Statistics in R" will take place on 19?22 January and is designed for complete beginners, combining clear explanations with hands-on practice in R. 
 
This course will help you read, interpret, and write R scripts, apply key statistical tools to research questions, and perform efficient, reproducible analyses. By the end, you'll confidently use R for both descriptive and inferential statistics.
 
Only a few seats are still available:  [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ ) 
 
Happy New Year everyone!
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Peer Reviewing and Editing Journals – last chance
messages.subject_clean: Peer Reviewing and Editing Journals – last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance to join our 1-day seminar Peer Reviewing and Editing Journals livestreaming January 8 with Carlton Fong (Department of Curriculum and Instruction, Texas State University). This seminar on peer reviewing and journal editing offers critical insights for refining your own epidemiological research, ensuring your R-based statistical analyses and data displays meet the highest standards of rigor and clarity for publication. By mastering efficient and constructive review practices, you can effectively assess the methodological soundness of submitted epidemiological studies, thereby safeguarding the integrity and advancing the quality of research in your field. Engaging in editorial service provides a powerful avenue to shape research agendas, promote best practices in epidemiological data analysis, and enhance your professional standing within the broader scientific community.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/from-peer-reviewing-to-editing-journals) to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Statistical Analysis of Social Network Data – last chance
messages.subject_clean: Statistical Analysis of Social Network Data – last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance to join our 1-day seminar Statistical Analysis of Social Network Data livestreaming January 15 with Tianxi Li (School of Statistics, University of Minnesota). This seminar on statistical analysis of social network data offers robust methods for researchers working with relational structures inherent in epidemiological contexts. Participants will learn to apply advanced R-based techniques for modeling disease transmission pathways, identifying influential nodes in health networks, and performing predictive analysis on attributed epidemiological data. These skills will enable the community to conduct more rigorous inference on complex relational data, enhance the reproducibility of their epidemiological studies, and explore novel research questions with confidence.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/statistical-analysis-of-social-network-d) to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Physalia Course Announcement: GIS Mapping and Spatial Analysis with R
messages.subject_clean: Physalia Course Announcement: GIS Mapping and Spatial Analysis with R
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce the upcoming Physalia online course Using R as a GIS: Mapping and Spatial Operations in R, scheduled for 2 to 6 March.
 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gis-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gis-in-r/ )
 
 
This course is designed for researchers and practitioners interested in spatial data analysis who wish to develop reproducible, script-based workflows in R.
Participants will gain a solid understanding of Geographic Information Systems (GIS) fundamentals, including cartographic projections and spatial data types, alongside practical skills to import, manage, and visualize spatial data in R. The course covers key vector and raster spatial operations, enabling automated mapping and spatial analyses of environmental and biodiversity data.
 
Live sessions will be held daily from 15:00 to 18:00 Berlin time, complemented by four hours of self-guided practical exercises with remote support available from 09:00 to 23:00 Berlin time to accommodate different time zones. 
.
For more information or to register, please contact us.
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Machine Learning for Computational Biology 2.0 – last chance
messages.subject_clean: Machine Learning for Computational Biology 2.0 – last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance to join our 1-day seminar Machine Learning for Computational Biology 2.0 livestreaming January 13 with Nikolay Oskolkov (Group Leader (PI) at LIOS). This workshop introduces machine learning techniques for analyzing complex datasets, offering advanced methods to complement traditional statistical approaches in epidemiological research. Participants will gain practical skills in R and Python to implement algorithms such as random forest, k-means clustering, and MCMC, which are invaluable for identifying disease patterns, predicting outcomes, and modeling complex risk factors. These hands-on capabilities will empower researchers to develop more sophisticated analytical tools and enhance the robustness of their epidemiological studies.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/machine-learning-for-computational-biolo) to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Causal Inference with AI and Machine Learning – last chance
messages.subject_clean: Causal Inference with AI and Machine Learning – last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance to join our 4-day seminar Causal Inference with AI and Machine Learning livestreaming January 30?February 2 with Jason Anastasopoulos (School of Public and International Affairs, University of Georgia). This seminar explores causal inference using modern machine learning and AI frameworks, with hands-on application in R. Researchers in epidemiology can leverage these advanced techniques to move beyond mere associations, establishing robust cause-and-effect relationships from complex observational data in their studies. The practical skills in R, combined with LLM-assisted diagnostics and visualization, will enhance the design, validity, and communication of findings for public health interventions and risk factor analyses.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/causal-inference-with-ai-and-machine-lea) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Online Course: Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R — April 2026
messages.subject_clean: Online Course: Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R — April 2026
messages.body_plain:
Dear r-sig-epi community,
 
Are you applying R in medical or biomedical research and want to deepen your understanding of biostatistics?
Are you looking to strengthen your data analysis skills and improve reproducibility in your workflows?
 
We invite you to join our Fundamentals of Biostatistics with R course, offered online from 13?17 April.
 
This five-day course offers a practical introduction to core statistical methods essential for medical and biomedical data analysis. Through hands-on sessions in R, you?ll learn to explore, analyse, and interpret datasets, focusing on applying appropriate tests, regression models, survival analysis, and reproducible reporting.
 
Course highlights:

Clear explanation of key biostatistical concepts

Hands-on coding using R for data visualisation and statistical tests

Linear and logistic regression, hypothesis testing, survival analysis

Emphasis on reproducibility and transparent reporting in R
 
Find more details and register here: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biostats-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/biostats-in-r/ ) 


Best regards,
Carlo 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Learn Machine Learning with R & tidymodels (16–20 Feb)
messages.subject_clean: Learn Machine Learning with R & tidymodels (16–20 Feb)
messages.body_plain:
Good morning all,
Are you interested in mastering machine learning for biological data?
There are a few seats left for our online course "Introduction to Machine Learning with R" (16?20 February).
Biology today generates complex multivariate data from multi-omics studies (genomics, proteomics, metabolomics, transcriptomics). Machine learning is a powerful tool to help analyze and interpret this data, alongside classical statistics.
In this course, you?ll get hands-on experience with multivariate methods and machine learning algorithms applied to biological datasets ? including practical training using the tidyverse-friendly tidymodels framework.
 
Course programme (all sessions 2?7 pm Berlin time):

ML basics and tidy ML with tidymodels

Supervised learning: regression, classification, model selection

Overfitting, resampling, model tuning

Tree-based methods: Random Forest, Boosting

Unsupervised learning: PCA, UMAP, Self-Organizing Maps
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course43/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course43/ ) 
 
Best regards,
Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] 2-day online course - Mastering Quarto CLI: From Authoring to Publishing - 5-6 February
messages.subject_clean: 2-day online course - Mastering Quarto CLI: From Authoring to Publishing - 5-6 February
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
Are you ready to discover the full potential of Quarto for creating sophisticated, reproducible documents and interactive publications?
 
Join us for Mastering Quarto CLI: From Authoring to Publishing, a 2-day online workshop designed to take you from beginner to confident Quarto user!
 
Course Dates: 5th?6th February 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/intro-to-quarto/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/intro-to-quarto/ ) 


In this comprehensive workshop, you will:

Learn how to install and set up Quarto and its command-line interface

Master Markdown fundamentals and Quarto-specific authoring features

Integrate R, Python, and Julia code seamlessly into your documents

Explore advanced project structures, theming, and multi-format publishing

Gain skills to publish your work professionally on platforms like GitHub Pages and Quarto Pub
Whether you?re a data scientist, researcher, or technical writer, this course will equip you with the tools and knowledge to produce polished, reproducible, and dynamic publications.
 
If you have any questions or need assistance, feel free to reach out.
Best regards, Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Foundations of Statistical Models and Linear Regression – last chance
messages.subject_clean: Foundations of Statistical Models and Linear Regression – last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance to join our 3-day seminar Foundations of Statistical Models and Linear Regression livestreaming February 3?5 with Centre for Applied Statistics Courses (CASC) (University College London). This seminar on Foundations of Statistical Models and Linear Regression offers comprehensive training in regression analysis, a fundamental tool for empirical research. Participants will gain practical expertise in applying various regression models, including logistic and Poisson, and learn diagnostic techniques crucial for robust data analysis using software like R. These skills will enable researchers to more effectively model complex epidemiological relationships, assess model quality, and communicate findings with greater precision in their studies.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/foundations-of-statistical-models-and-li) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Introduction to Mixed Methods Research: From Theory to Practice 2.0 – last chance
messages.subject_clean: Introduction to Mixed Methods Research: From Theory to Practice 2.0 – last chance
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance to join our 31-day seminar Introduction to Mixed Methods Research: From Theory to Practice 2.0 livestreaming February 9?March 11 with Sergi Fabregues (Department of Psychology and Education, Universitat Oberta de Catalunya and Mixed Methods Program, University of Michigan). This seminar offers a practical introduction to mixed methods research, providing a robust framework for integrating qualitative and quantitative approaches. Epidemiologists leveraging R for statistical analysis can apply these principles to enrich their studies, gaining deeper insights into complex health outcomes by combining numerical data with contextual understanding. Participants will learn to formulate comprehensive research questions, select appropriate designs, and employ effective integration strategies, enabling them to produce more nuanced and impactful epidemiological findings.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/introduction-to-mixed-methods-research-f-1) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
